{{Infobox character
| name = Emperor (The Elder Empire: Sea)
| aliases = The Emperor (The Elder Empire: Sea)
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|levitate wagons, create doors in blank walls}}{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|, Read Elders}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Emperor}}{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|, father of all}}{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|, [[Sadesthenes]]}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Guild of Magisters]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|son of a wine merchant}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Imperial Palace}}
| appears_in = Elder Empire
| introduced = [[Of Sea and Shadow (novel)|Of Sea and Shadow]]
}}

''See also: [[Emperor (The Elder Empire: Sea) Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor was the long-reigning, immortal leader of the [[Elder Empire]], a figure whose rule spanned nearly two millennia. Known as [[Kelarac]], he was a unique entity who functioned similarly to a [[Great Elder]] by utilizing their power to govern humanity. Unlike other [[Elders]], he possessed no specific domain and instead traveled through various territories. His primary motivation was the pursuit of absolute knowledge, a goal that drove him to learn [[Reading]] in Ach’magut. While his desire for information meant the survival of humans was not his priority, he served as a vital protector, using his strength and specialized techniques to defend civilization against the incursions of the [[Elders]]. His authority was absolute, backed by an iron will and a capacity to command even the most powerful subjects. He utilized a throne designed to amplify [[Reading]], though he ultimately refused to use it for communicating with [[Great Elders]] due to the risk of mental destruction. In his final years, [[the Emperor]]'s mental state declined as he struggled against the awakening of the [[Dead Mother]]. He eventually died, an event some suspect he permitted, leaving a power vacuum that led to increased Elder activity. Following his death, the [[Aurelian Empire]] sought to stabilize itself by appointing [[Calder]] [[Marten]] as the [[Imperial Steward]] to lead and protect the people in his absence.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor was a man who lived for nearly two millennia, serving as the light and life of the Empire while performing many bad deeds during his reign. A powerful Reader who borrowed the strength of the [[Elders]], he used objects to harbor his limitless [[Intent]] and utilized a parasite to achieve immortality. His long life was marked by abilities such as levitating wagons or creating doors in walls, yet he ultimately died over five years ago because he could not resist the awakening of the [[Dead Mother]]. Though his position is currently vacant, rumors persist regarding his resurrection, and it is believed that holding the Heart could establish a claim to his throne.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor has been dead for over five years.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c1">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor is deceased.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c3">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor is a Reader with overwhelming powers.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c4">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor can levitate wagons or create doors in blank walls.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor lived for almost two millennia.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor is a man who uses objects that harbor his limitless [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c6">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor used a parasite to achieve immortality.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor is believed to be resurrected or immortal.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor is a figure Naberius lived around in the [[Imperial]] Palace.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c12">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Naberius has had long proximity to [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c14">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The position of Emperor is currently vacant.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor is described as the light and life of the Empire.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c15">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The future Emperor is expected to board the ship.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c16">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor died because he could not resist the [[Dead Mother]] when she began to awaken.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor was a powerful man who borrowed the power of the [[Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c20">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor has done many bad things in his time.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Holding the Heart can make a case for [[the Emperor]]’s throne.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor is described as being so removed from humanity that he might as well be [[Elderspawn]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|The Emperor ordered [[Alsa Grayweather]] to be kept in [[Guild]] custody.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor is a figure of absolute power and authority, often regarded as the father of all, who serves as an exception to the rule that [[Readers]] should not rule over a populace. While he possesses no official duties and acts as a figurehead capable of indulging his whims, he makes numerous decisions for the [[Guilds]] despite having no concern for individual people. Historically, [[the Emperor]] utilized the [[Optasia]] to control amplification devices and project his [[Intent]] instantly, though he eventually sealed the power away and built a global network of statue relays. In the current era, [[Calder]] is destined to become [[the Emperor]] and has already been spared from execution by the throne. The [[Imperial Steward]] invokes [[the Emperor]]'s authority to issue orders, such as searching for suspicious vessels or sending Navigators with the [[Imperial Seal]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The characters discuss the need for an Emperor.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c2">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor spared [[Calder]] from execution.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c3">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor used the [[Optasia]] to control amplification devices and use his [[Intent]] anywhere instantly.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor cannot grow living flesh at will.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] is an Emperor who intends to sit on a throne.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor makes many decisions for the [[Guilds]] but does not care about people.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c7">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The original Emperor is considered the father of all.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c8">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor sends Navigators with personal instructions and the [[Imperial Seal]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c9">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor is the only one to have seen Ach’magut directly.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] is [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Calder]] is destined to be [[the Emperor]] one day.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor is an exception to the rule that [[Readers]] should not rule over the populace.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor is a figure of authority that people are encouraged to protect.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor built a network of relays consisting of statues that look like him all around the world.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor sealed the [[Optasia]] away from himself and allegedly employed watchers to ensure he never used it.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor specified the construction of the carriage [[Calder]] is riding in.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c18">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor has invested thousands of [[goldmarks]] into [[The Testament]]'s construction.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c19">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor's authority is invoked by the [[Imperial Steward]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c20">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor is issuing orders to search for suspicious vessels.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c21">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor is a figurehead with absolute power to indulge his whims.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c23">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The Emperor has no official duties.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c23" />}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Once known as the self-educated son of a wine merchant named [[Sadesthenes]], [[the Emperor]] lived for over two thousand years while protecting all people from the [[Elders]]. He possessed an iron will forged in war and used his powers to invest relics, armor, and even his bed with [[Intent]], often appearing in trademark attire featuring a wide-brimmed hat and a crown. Claiming to be the first Reader, he asserted his authority over the [[Guild of Magisters]] and eventually chose to give up his life rather than lose a battle to the [[Elders]]. Following his death, which drew massive crowds to the Capital for his funeral, he left behind a powerful personality echo within the [[Optasia]] to bolster his will against [[Nakothi]]'s corruption. This formidable echo, crafted by layering memories into his belongings, resulted in [[the Emperor]] and [[Calder]] waking within the same body.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor has powers that can be referenced by [[Imperial relics]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c2">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor protected everyone from the [[Elders]] and has lived for over two thousand years.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c4">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor had a desire for safe, peaceful sleep that invested his bed with [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor's crown was once made of bronze.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c5">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor's trademark attire includes a wide-brimmed hat.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Regents]] trust in the power of [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c9">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Calder]] has felt the force of will projected by [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c11">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor has a crown.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor has a ghost.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor invested the armor to protect and preserve the life of the wearer.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Capital swelled with people for the funeral of [[the Emperor]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor's images are painted on walls in the Capital to protect spirits.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c21">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Defying [[the Emperor]] is considered defying the Empire, civilization, and humanity.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor has [[Read]] [[Elders]] before.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor claims to be the first Reader and asserts that the [[Guild of Magisters]] has no authority over his teachings.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor crafts a ghost by layering his memories into his belongings and molding his [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor possesses boundless resolve and an iron will forged in war.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor gave up his life rather than losing a battle to the [[Elders]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c24">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor left a clear, comprehensive [[Intent]] in the [[Optasia]] to create an echo of his personality.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor's personality echo was intended to bolster his will against [[Nakothi]]'s corruption.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor was once known by the name [[Sadesthenes]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor's persona as [[Sadesthenes]] was the self-educated son of a wine merchant.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor and [[Calder]] woke in the same body.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The Emperor's echo is a force to be reckoned with.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|“Nobody wants to be the Emperor?” Calder spat out.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c14" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|“The future Emperor is boarding,” the Guard said simply.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c16" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|“Guild Head, we were ordered by the Emperor himself to keep her in Guild custody.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c28" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|“I am the first Reader. The Guild of Magisters has no authority over my teachings.”<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c22" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|“Sadesthenes.” The Emperor gave a small, fond smile.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c25" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire characters]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Readers]]
